LA LAGUNA - MYSTERIES AND LEGENDS IN THE WORLD HERITAGE CITY
This walking route through the UNESCO World Heritage city of San Cristóbal de La Laguna combines
history, architecture, and local legends.
ROUTE TYPE: Self-guided walking tour (historic city center). Lineal.
ESTIMATED DURATION: 2–2.5 hours (including visits)
DISTANCE: Approx. 2–2.5 km
ROUTE OVERVIEW (STOPS):
1. Church of Nuestra Señora de la Concepción
2. Casa Lercaro (Museum of History and Anthropology)
3. Cathedral of San Cristóbal de La Laguna
4. Calle Obispo Rey Redondo (La Carrera)
5. Nava Palace
6. Plaza del Adelantado
7. Old Convent of Santo Domingo
VISITABLE PLACES & OPENING HOURS:
(󽁔󽁕󽁖Indicative schedules based on typical current information. Subject to change—always check
official or local sources before visiting.)
• Church of Nuestra Señora de la Concepción: usually open daily (approx. 09:00–18:00).
Tower visits may have separate hours.
• Casa Lercaro (Museum of History and Anthropology): Tuesday to Saturday 09:00–20:00, and
Sunday, Monday and public holidays 10:00–17:00.
• Cathedral of San Cristóbal de La Laguna. Generally open daily: approx. 09:00–18:00
(restricted during services).
• Calle Obispo Rey Redondo & historic houses: public street (free access). Some buildings
like Casa de los Capitanes may be visitable during municipal hours.
• Nava Palace: exterior visit only (interior usually not open to the public).
• Plaza del Adelantado & surroundings (Town Hall, Monastery of Santa Catalina): public
space always open. Convent visits are very limited (not generally open except special
dates).
• Old Convent of Santo Domingo: opening varies depending on exhibitions/events (generally
cultural use; check locally).
PRACTICAL TIPS FOR THE ROUTE:
• Bring a light jacket: La Laguna is known for its humidity and sudden temperature drops.
• Wear comfortable shoes: Streets are cobbled and best explored on foot.
• Take your time indoors: Museums and churches add depth to the experience.
• Enjoy local life: stop at cafés and bars along the route—part of La Laguna’s charm.
• Respect sacred and historic spaces: Some sites are active religious or cultural venues.
This route offers a compact yet rich journey through one of Tenerife’s most historic cities, where
Renaissance urban planning, colonial heritage, and centuries-old legends blend into a unique
walking experience.
